401.description: Magic-link / JWT invalid, expired or consumed.
Magic-link / JWT invalid, expired or consumed.
Email + password combination did not match.

post.description:
No-op on the server side — the dashboard clears the cookie.

A future iteration can persist a token-revocation list for hard
logout semantics; for now the 24h sliding access TTL acts as the
bound on credential lifetime.

get.description:
Resolve a tenant by its ``primary_domain``.

Public endpoint used by the dashboard edge middleware to inject branding
tokens before rendering any RSC. Returns 404 with stable code
``TENANT_UNRESOLVED`` if no active tenant matches.

404.description: Tenant could not be resolved for the supplied host.

409.description: Slug already in use by another tenant.

schema.title: Response List Tenants Api V1 Tenants Get

404.description: Tenant id does not exist.

post.description:
Create a new catalog item for the resolved tenant.

The item starts in the ``status`` given by the payload (default
``draft``). ``slug`` must be unique within the tenant — a collision
returns ``409 CONTENT_SLUG_TAKEN``. Requires the admin/operator role.

409.description: Slug already in use for this tenant.

get.description:
Paginated operator listing of the tenant's catalog across **all**
statuses (including ``draft`` and ``archived``). For the public,
published-only, filterable grid use ``GET /public/catalog`` instead.

get.summary: Get a content item by slug
get.description:
Fetch a single item by its human-readable slug, scoped to the
resolved tenant. Returns ``404 CONTENT_NOT_FOUND`` when no item with
that slug exists for the tenant.

404.description: Content item not found for this tenant.

get.summary: Get a content item by id
get.description:
Fetch a single item by its id, scoped to the resolved tenant.
Returns ``404 CONTENT_NOT_FOUND`` when the id does not belong to the
tenant.

404.description: Content item not found for this tenant.

patch.description:
Partial update. Cannot mutate ``id``, ``tenant_id`` or
``created_at`` (they are absent from :class:`ContentItemUpdate`).
Slug changes re-check the ``(tenant_id, slug)`` uniqueness constraint
before flush so we surface a stable 409 instead of a 500.

404.description: Content item not found for this tenant.

409.description: Slug already in use for this tenant.

delete.description:
Soft delete — transitions ``status`` to ``archived`` (the row is
preserved). Idempotent: deleting an already-archived row is a no-op
that still returns 204 and writes an audit entry.

404.description: Content item not found for this tenant.

post.description:
Move ``draft|scheduled|archived`` → ``published``. Returns 409
``CONTENT_ALREADY_PUBLISHED`` when the row is already in the
``published`` state so callers can display a stable error.

On publish, fans out a "new title" inbox notification to content
subscribers (🔔 Notificar) and followers of any credited author — in
the SAME transaction (idempotent per member+content). Push/email
delivery is scheduled best-effort AFTER commit so it never blocks or
rolls back the publish.

404.description: Content item not found for this tenant.

409.description: Item is already published — publish is a no-op.

post.description:
Upload a downloadable asset (PDF / EPUB / MOBI / MP3 / M4A) for a
content item. The file lands at the canonical storage key
``items/{item_id}/file.{format}`` so the DOWNLOAD delivery strategy
can return a signed URL to entitled members without further wiring.

Re-uploading the same format overwrites the previous payload (the
storage key is deterministic) and bumps ``metadata.assets[format]``
on the content item. With ``chapter=N`` (AUD-05) the audio lands at
``items/{id}/audio_ch_{N}.{format}`` and bumps ``metadata.assets["audio_ch_{N}"]``
so delivery signs the chapter's own object (and charges the per-chapter gate).

400.description: The tenant's delivery strategy or the content kind cannot consume single-file uploads in the requested format.

404.description: Content item not found for this tenant.

413.description: Uploaded file exceeds the configured size limit.

502.description: Object storage refused or failed the write — usually a credentials / policy misconfiguration on the bucket.

put.summary: Set the audiobook narrator + chapter list (merge-preserve)
put.description:
Upsert the audiobook block (``narrator`` + ``audio_chapters``) on an
item's ``metadata_`` WITHOUT touching any other key (D-1 merge-preserve).

A plain ``PATCH /{id}`` with ``metadata`` replaces the whole blob and
would drop ``assets``/``cover`` — a data-loss trap. This endpoint reads
the current metadata under a row lock, sets only the two audio keys and
writes it back in one transaction (R-5), so an interleaved asset upload
of the same row cannot be lost.

The public detail derives ``audio{narrator,total,chapters}`` from these
keys (``_derive_item_audio``); delivery gates playback per chapter.

404.description: Content item not found for this tenant.

422.description: Chapter orders are duplicated or not contiguous 1..N.

post.description:
Upload a public cover image for a content item.

The file lands at ``images/{tenant_id}/items/{item_id}/cover.{ext}``
on the public-read storage prefix; the resulting public URL is
persisted on ``content_items.cover_image_url`` so catalog responses
can surface it without a per-request signed URL roundtrip.
Re-uploading replaces the previous payload (the storage key is
deterministic on the file extension).

Allowed Content-Types: ``image/jpeg``, ``image/png``, ``image/webp``.

400.description: Content-Type is not a supported cover image type.

404.description: Content item not found for this tenant.

413.description: Cover image exceeds the configured size limit.

502.description: Object storage refused or failed the cover write.

delete.description:
Remove the cover image — clears the column and deletes the
storage object. Idempotent: deleting an item that has no cover
returns 204 and still records an audit entry.

404.description: Content item not found for this tenant.

502.description: Object storage refused or failed the cover write.

delete.description:
Remove one downloadable format from a content item (#122).

Deletes the storage object and drops ``metadata.assets[fmt]`` so the
public download page stops offering it. Idempotent: deleting a format
the item never had returns 204. Does NOT touch ``media_title_id`` —
the per-format assets share the item's single Media title, so a
single-format delete leaves the others (and delivery's local fallback)
intact.

400.description: Format or Content-Type is not supported for downloadable assets.

404.description: Content item not found for this tenant.

502.description: Object storage refused or failed the write — usually a credentials / policy misconfiguration on the bucket.

get.description:
Public reviews for an item by id, in the dashboard's flat shape.

``average``/``count`` come from the ``content_items`` denorm kept in
sync by ``ReviewRepository._refresh_denorm``. ``author_name`` is the
reviewer's display_name (neutral label when unset — never PII).

404.description: Content item not found for this tenant.

post.description:
Member-scoped review upsert by item id (idempotent per member).

Mirrors ``POST /me/reviews`` (reuses ``ReviewRepository.upsert`` and
its denorm refresh) but takes the content id from the path so the
dashboard's item page posts without threading it through the body.
The review is written with the server-derived tenant + member ids —
no client-supplied tenant/member, so no cross-tenant IDOR.

403.description: The member token's tenant does not match the request tenant.

404.description: Content item not found for this tenant.

post.description:
Move any state → ``archived``. Idempotent: re-archiving returns
200 with the unchanged row and still records an audit entry.

404.description: Content item not found for this tenant.

get.description:
Authoritative entitlement read for content gating.

Defaults to the local SQL ``entitlements`` table — the canonical
cache the inbound webhook router writes to. ``?source=provider``
forces a live call to the configured entitlement provider (debug
only; not used in hot delivery path).

post.description:
Force-refresh entitlement state from the upstream provider.

Mitigates the redirect race after checkout: the fan returns to Atlas
before the provider's webhook has been delivered. The dashboard
calls this endpoint on ``?refreshed=1``; it queries the provider
once and persists the row locally so the next ``has_active`` read
returns true immediately. Idempotent — repeat calls are cheap.

Create a checkout link for the calling member.

Tenant-aware (D-18): resolves the entitlement provider from the tenant's
``config.payments`` (kind/base_url) + env-namespaced secret, and maps the
requested ``plan_ref`` (a ``config.plans`` slug) to the provider's product
id via ``plan_product_map`` before delegating. Carries Atlas's
``tenant_id`` / ``scope`` / ``scope_ref`` so the provider can echo them
back on the resulting webhook (those fields drive
``EntitlementRepository.grant`` once the payment confirms). A ``mock``
provider returns a fake URL (no real charge).

Anonymous checkout for a not-yet-signed-in buyer (web ``/vip``).

The buyer supplies only an email — the card is entered on the provider's
hosted page, never here. Flow: create-or-reuse a tenant-scoped member by
email (idempotent), commit it so a racing webhook finds the row, then
delegate to the same provider ``create_checkout_link`` as the member
checkout with ``member.id`` as the external id. The provider echoes that
id back on the paid webhook, so the grant attributes to the right member
(server-authoritative — this endpoint NEVER writes an Entitlement; only
the confirmed-payment webhook grants). Rate-limited per browser and
returns an identical ``{url}`` whether the member was created or reused,
so it can't be used to probe which emails exist. A ``mock`` provider
returns a fake URL (no real charge).

422.description: Invalid return_url (must be https or site-relative).

post.description:
Activate the tenant's free trial for the calling member (D-14).

Grants ``Entitlement(scope="plan", scope_ref=trial.plan_ref,
expires_at=now+days, granted_by="trial")`` which unlocks like VIP until it
passively expires (no revoke job). Server-authoritative: the app never
grants the trial. Dedup (once-per-member) by ANY existing
``granted_by="trial"`` row for the (tenant, member, plan) — even an
expired one — so the trial cannot be re-consumed (R-6).

Returns ``granted=false`` with a stable reason when the tenant has no
enabled trial (``trial_unavailable``) or the member already used it
(``trial_already_used``).

schema.title: Response By Member Api V1 Entitlements By Member  Member Id  Get

404.description: Entitlement id does not exist for this tenant.

get.description:
Mint a playback ticket for the requested item.

Decision order:

1. **Operator bypass** — admin/operator skip the entitlement check
   and any strategy-level quota / format guard. Audit row written
   so the action is traceable.
2. **Has active entitlement** — non-operator caller proceeds to
   strategy resolution.
3. **Lazy auto-grant** — caller has none; if the tenant qualifies
   under the free-tier predicate, attempt the grant inline and
   re-check. Closes the gap when eager grant on sign-in failed.
4. **403 ENTITLEMENT_DENIED** — fall through to subscription wall.

Chapter gate selection (F3 — cross-quota bypass fix): a validated
``?chapter=`` request is charged against the per-chapter quota
(``audio_chapters_per_day``) ONLY when it resolves to a real
per-chapter asset (``metadata.assets["audio_ch_{order}"]``). When the
chapter falls back to the FULL audio file (mocks-first, L-28), the
delivered artifact is the whole title, so the request is charged
against the per-TITLE gate (``books_per_day``, including its
``keeps_unlocked`` semantics) — the slot spent always matches what is
delivered. Otherwise a free member who exhausted ``books_per_day``
could keep extracting complete audiobooks via ``?chapter=1``, and a
tenant with ``audio_chapters_per_day > books_per_day`` would hand out
MORE full files per day than the title gate allows.

parameters.name: format

schema.description: Optional download format hint (pdf|epub|mobi|mp3|m4a). When omitted, the strategy picks the first uploaded format from ``content_item.metadata.assets`` or the strategy default.
schema.title: Format

parameters.description: Optional download format hint (pdf|epub|mobi|mp3|m4a). When omitted, the strategy picks the first uploaded format from ``content_item.metadata.assets`` or the strategy default.

parameters.name: chapter

schema.description: Optional audiobook chapter (1-based ``order`` from the item's ``metadata.audio_chapters``). 404 ``CHAPTER_NOT_FOUND`` when the order does not exist. The chapter asset is ``metadata.assets['audio_ch_{order}']`` when uploaded; otherwise the URL derives from the full audio asset (same file — mocks-first, L-28) and the app seeks using the chapter offsets.
schema.title: Chapter

parameters.description: Optional audiobook chapter (1-based ``order`` from the item's ``metadata.audio_chapters``). 404 ``CHAPTER_NOT_FOUND`` when the order does not exist. The chapter asset is ``metadata.assets['audio_ch_{order}']`` when uploaded; otherwise the URL derives from the full audio asset (same file — mocks-first, L-28) and the app seeks using the chapter offsets.

402.description: Free member crossed a freemium gate without the required plan or exhausted the daily distinct-title quota — a subscription is required.

403.description: Requested format is not in the tenant's allow-list.

404.description: Requested format is not available for this item (e.g. it was deleted), or the requested chapter order does not exist in the item's audio chapters.

post.summary: Ingest a batch of app usage events
post.description: Accepts a batch (≤50) of provider-agnostic usage events and dispatches the accepted ones to the tenant's active measurement tool. Anonymous allowed; capture off / out-of-allowlist returns `accepted: 0`.

Inbound ``title.*`` from 1Platform Media. Verifies HMAC against each
local tenant's ``config.media_supply.webhook_secret`` (N:1 fan-out) and
runs the per-storefront auto-publish (G-B1). A payload that no configured
secret verifies is silently ignored (no recipient) — never 500s.

401.description: No configured tenant secret verified the signature (WEBHOOK_SIGNATURE_INVALID).

post.description:
Upload the tenant favicon and persist ``branding.favicon_url``.

The file lands at ``images/{tenant_id}/branding/favicon.{ext}`` on
the public-read storage prefix; the resulting public URL is merged
onto the branding group (tenant_settings row + ``tenants.config``
mirror) so both the admin editor and the public resolver see it.
Re-uploading replaces the previous payload. Operators who host the
favicon elsewhere can keep setting ``branding.favicon_url`` via
PATCH ``/admin/settings`` instead.

Allowed Content-Types: ``image/x-icon``,
``image/vnd.microsoft.icon``, ``image/png``, ``image/jpeg``,
``image/webp``.

413.description: Favicon exceeds the configured size cap.

502.description: Object storage rejected the favicon payload.

409.description: Slug already in use by another tenant.

schema.title: Response List Tenants Api V1 Admin Tenants Get

404.description: Tenant id does not exist.
Tenant id does not exist.

422.description: A secondary archetype duplicates the immutable primary archetype.

404.description: Tenant id does not exist.

patch.description:
Replace ``kind_paths`` and/or ``legacy_items_redirect``.

Renames are alias-preserving: the previous segment lives on as a
legacy 301 target for :data:`KIND_PATH_ALIAS_TTL_DAYS` days. The
scheduler is expected to GC expired entries (out of scope for this
handler).

404.description: Tenant id does not exist.

get.description:
Paginated audit log read with optional filter params.

Filters are AND-combined: ``actor_id`` narrows by mutator,
``method`` by HTTP verb, ``path_prefix`` does a LIKE-prefix match
on the canonical path, and ``from`` / ``to`` bound the
``occurred_at`` range. Tenant scoping is always applied first.

schema.title: Response List Audit Log Api V1 Admin Audit Log Get

get.description:
Recent audit log rows for the dashboard activity feed.

DESC-ordered by ``occurred_at`` (the repo's default ordering).
Capped at ``_RECENT_LIMIT_MAX`` so the dashboard widget can't
accidentally page through the full log via this route.

schema.title: Response List Recent Api V1 Admin Audit Log Recent Get

post.description:
Apply ``action`` to every item id under this tenant.

Each item is processed in isolation so a single 404/conflict does
not abort the rest. The response carries the count of successes
plus the per-id failure reasons for the dashboard's bulk-bar
error chip.

get.description:
Paginated catalog list for the admin table.

Filters are AND-combined. ``q`` searches title / slug / metadata
ISBN with a parameterised ILIKE — the upcoming Postgres typeahead
GIN (migration 0021) will graft on without a route change since the
parameters are bound, never string-formatted. ``reads_30d`` is a
LEFT JOIN over ``member_progress``; rows without progress show 0.

Return the editable detail payload for a single content item.

Embeds the rolling-30d metrics block so the admin edit screen does
not need a second round trip to render its sparkline + completion
gauge.

patch.description:
Optimistic-concurrency PATCH on a content item.

The ``If-Match`` header carries the version the client last saw.
Missing header AND mismatched version both raise
:class:`OptimisticLockError` (HTTP 409 ``OPTIMISTIC_LOCK_FAILED``)
so the dashboard branches deterministically: refetch + retry. The
actual write is an atomic UPDATE WHERE version = expected so a
concurrent winner cannot be silently overwritten.

get.description:
Paginated listing of storage objects referenced by content items.

v1 derives the listing from ``content_items.metadata.assets`` plus
the ``cover_image_url`` column — bucket-side discovery is deferred
(cut API-C6). ``kind`` filters by content_type prefix when supplied.

Read the tenant's media-supply integration — flags only, never the
secret values (the sealed ``api_key``/``webhook_secret`` stay server-side).

patch.description:
Write the tenant's media-supply integration. Secrets are sealed with
Fernet at rest; an omitted secret preserves the stored ciphertext.

404.description: Tenant not found (TENANT_NOT_FOUND).

delete.description:
Remove a storage object after verifying ``used_in == 0``.

The 409 ``MEDIA_STILL_USED`` is a guard against orphaning a
published item — the dashboard surfaces it as "Detach from N items
first" so the operator does not accidentally break a live page.

404.description: No content item under this tenant references the key.

409.description: Asset is still referenced; refuse to delete.

get.description:
Return the admin KPI block + readings chart + top-items widget.

Postgres serves the materialised view; SQLite (and the
pre-first-refresh Postgres window) falls back to direct queries.
The full response is cached per tenant for ``_KPIS_CACHE_TTL_SECONDS``
so the dashboard home stays cheap even under tab-spam refresh.

Top-N read items in the requested rolling window.

"Reads" is defined as the count of ``member_progress`` rows touched
inside the window — the same signal that drives MAU. Deltas (vs
the prior window) are placeholders set to 0 in v1 (see
REDESIGN_PLAN.md cut API-C1 + the MV deferred deltas).

Day-bucketed counts for ``metric`` over the last ``days`` days.

Buckets are computed in Python after a single tenant-scoped fetch
so the call stays portable across Postgres and SQLite without
dialect-specific date_trunc syntax. Tenants larger than ~10k
progress rows / day should switch to a tenant-scoped MV in a
follow-up.

get.summary: App telemetry KPIs / funnel / timeseries (active measurement tool)
get.description: Tenant-scoped app analytics read from the active measurement tool. `metric=kpis` → DAU/MAU/reads/downloads/conversions; `metric=funnel` → paywall_view → paywall_cta → conversion; `metric=timeseries` → zero-filled daily counts for one `event`. Conversions derive from entitlements (local). Fail-soft: unconfigured/flaky tool → zeros.

schema.title: Response List Publishers Api V1 Admin Publishers Get
Response List Redirects Api V1 Admin Redirects Get

get.description:
Return the tenant's SEO health snapshot.

The tenant's ``canonical_host`` is resolved from
``tenant.config.seo.canonical_host`` so the dashboard can hint
which property the GSC provider should query against. Mock
provider ignores it; the real provider uses it as the GSC
property key.

``?refresh=1`` bypasses the Valkey cache and forces a live fetch
from the underlying provider. The fresh snapshot is written back
to the cache so the next operator load is fast.

post.description:
Evict every cached SEO snapshot for the tenant.

The operator triggers this after editing ``tenant.config.seo.canonical_host``
so the next ``GET /admin/seo-health`` hits the live provider with
the new property.

schema.title: Response List Libraries Api V1 Admin Libraries Get
Response List Tenant Subscriptions Api V1 Admin Tenants  Tenant Id  Library Subscriptions Get
Response Retry Sync Job Api V1 Admin Sync Jobs  Job Id  Retry Post

400.description: Posted ids do not match the stored rail set.

404.description: No rail with that id for this tenant.
No rail with that id for this tenant.
No plan with that id for this tenant.
No plan with that id for this tenant.

409.description: A member with that email already exists for this tenant.

404.description: No member with that id for this tenant.

get.description:
Newest-first inbox page for the authenticated member.

``unread_count`` is computed over the WHOLE inbox (not just the
returned page) so the app badge stays truthful when older unread
rows fall outside ``limit``.

post.description:
Mark one notification read. Ownership is enforced in the UPDATE's
WHERE clause (tenant + member), so the mutation and the check are a
single statement — no read-then-write race.

404.description: Notification does not exist for this member/tenant — a foreign id is indistinguishable from a missing one.

schema.title: Response Decision Api V1 Ads Decision  Content Id  Get

get.description:
Resolve the live stream detail block for the dashboard player.

v1 maps ``{stream_id}`` to a ``live_schedule_slots.channel_id`` —
each tenant exposes channels as the source of truth, and the
current program is the slot whose ``[start_at, end_at)`` window
contains "now". A 404 is returned when no slot has ever been
scheduled for that channel under this tenant; that doubles as a
cross-tenant guard.

The ``manifest_url`` is synthesised from the channel id; v1 leaves
the actual playback wiring to the transcoding provider (see
``app/services/providers/transcoding/``). ``viewers_count`` is a
placeholder until the streaming counter pipeline lands.

404.description: Live channel not found for this tenant.

schema.title: Response Now Api V1 Live Now Get

get.description:
Return the authenticated member's profile + active plan slug.

``timezone`` is hard-coded to ``UTC`` in v1 (no DB column yet);
the field shape is preserved so the dashboard does not need a
migration when timezone storage lands.

patch.description:
Partial update of the member's profile.

``name`` writes ``display_name``; ``locale`` writes
``preferred_locale``. ``timezone`` is accepted but not persisted
in v1 (no column yet); the request still validates so the
dashboard does not have to gate writes on a feature flag.

get.description:
Return the authenticated member's persisted devices ("sessions").

Backed by the ``member_devices`` table (Stream G): every device that
signed in with a fingerprint is a revocable row. The active request is
flagged ``is_current`` when ``device_fingerprint`` matches a persisted
row; otherwise the current device is surfaced as a synthetic leading
entry so the panel always shows at least the device in use (and web
members who signed in without a fingerprint still see one session).
``ip_redacted`` is only known for the live request — persisted rows
report ``"unknown"`` (no IP is stored). Every query is scoped by
``(tenant_id, member_id)`` from the auth context.

parameters.name: device_fingerprint

schema.description: The caller's own device fingerprint (as stored at sign-in). When supplied, the matching persisted device is flagged ``is_current`` and is preserved by ``DELETE /me/sessions`` (revoke others). Optional — omitted ⇒ the current device is surfaced as a synthetic entry derived from the request.
schema.title: Device Fingerprint

parameters.description: The caller's own device fingerprint (as stored at sign-in). When supplied, the matching persisted device is flagged ``is_current`` and is preserved by ``DELETE /me/sessions`` (revoke others). Optional — omitted ⇒ the current device is surfaced as a synthetic entry derived from the request.

delete.description:
Revoke every device except the current one ("sign out everywhere else").

Deletes all of the member's ``member_devices`` rows except the one whose
fingerprint matches ``device_fingerprint`` (the caller's current device).
When no fingerprint is supplied, every device row is removed. Idempotent
and scoped by ``(tenant_id, member_id)``.

parameters.name: device_fingerprint

schema.description: The caller's own device fingerprint (as stored at sign-in). When supplied, the matching persisted device is flagged ``is_current`` and is preserved by ``DELETE /me/sessions`` (revoke others). Optional — omitted ⇒ the current device is surfaced as a synthetic entry derived from the request.
schema.title: Device Fingerprint

parameters.description: The caller's own device fingerprint (as stored at sign-in). When supplied, the matching persisted device is flagged ``is_current`` and is preserved by ``DELETE /me/sessions`` (revoke others). Optional — omitted ⇒ the current device is surfaced as a synthetic entry derived from the request.

delete.description:
Revoke a single device session ("sign out from this device").

Deletes the member's ``member_devices`` row scoped by
``(tenant_id, member_id, id)``. A malformed id or one that belongs to
another member/tenant returns 404 (never 403) so a caller cannot probe
which session ids exist. The JWT itself is stateless and short-lived, so
revocation removes the device record (and stops future re-recognition);
a hard token-revocation list is deferred.

404.description: Session not registered for this member.

get.description:
Return the member's continue-watching / continue-reading rail.

``status=in_progress`` filters to rows with ``finished_at IS NULL``;
``status=finished`` returns the inverse. Default ordering is most
recent first by ``last_at``, capped at ``limit`` (default 20).
Payload is enriched with ``title``, ``slug``, ``cover_url`` and
``kind`` via a single SELECT against ``content_items`` so the
dashboard can render posters/titles instead of UUIDs (#69).

Return the member's download history rail.

Ordered most-recent first by ``downloaded_at``. Paginated via
opaque ``cursor`` (keyset over ``(downloaded_at, id)``). Items
are enriched with ``title``/``cover_url``/``slug``/``kind`` to
keep the UX consistent with the Continuar rail.

Return one summary per non-library watchlist bucket.

Backs the Listas tab in ``/me/library``: surfaces Favoritos, Ver
más tarde and Lista de lectura with item counts and a preview
of up to three items each. The default ``library`` bucket stays
on the Watchlist tab and is intentionally excluded.

patch.description:
Last-write-wins upsert of a member's progress row.

``last_at`` is bumped to "now" on every call. ``finished=true``
sets ``finished_at`` to "now"; ``finished=false`` clears it; the
parameter is optional (omitted = leave alone).

post.description:
Idempotent review CRUD: ``(member_id, content_id)`` is unique so
the second POST updates the existing row rather than 409-ing.

The denorm on ``content_items.rating_avg/rating_count`` is refreshed
in the same transaction (see ``ReviewRepository._refresh_denorm``).

Persist the member's intent to be notified about a content drop.

v1 records the (member, content, channels) tuple; the actual
delivery (email / push) is wired post-redesign. Idempotent
behaviour: re-subscribing replaces the channel list rather than
409-ing so the dashboard can switch channels without a second
DELETE roundtrip.

get.description:
Return the member's active plan-scope subscription, or null.

Derived from the entitlements ledger: scope='plan' grants where
expires_at is in the future (or null for comp/family plans). The
payment method is reported only when an upstream provider has
surfaced it via webhook metadata — Atlas itself never holds card
data.

Return the member's effective freemium policy (AFR-01).

Crosses ``config.app.freemium`` × the member's entitlements × the daily
distinct-title counter to produce the meter (``used/remaining/reset_at``),
the reader preview cap (``preview_pages``), the trial CTA state, and the
active subscription. UX only — the binding gate is server-side delivery
(D-3). ``enabled=false`` ⇒ everything allowed.

Most recently awarded milestones for the dashboard's F8
"Logros recientes" card. Emission is driven by the
``upsert_progress`` route via ``emit_for_progress_write``; this
endpoint is read-only.

The member's full achievement catalog with locked/unlocked state.

Unlike ``/achievements/recent`` (only awarded rows), this returns every
achievement the tenant offers — so the app shows locked goals instead
of an empty grid when nothing is earned yet (atlas-app #10). The
presentation (label/emoji/description) is tenant-configurable via
``config.achievements``; unlocked state is merged from the member's
awarded ledger by ``kind``.

post.description: Idempotently store the device's push handle for the authenticated member. A repeated POST of the same token refreshes it (200) instead of creating a duplicate. The tenant + member are taken from the auth context — the body never carries them.

delete.description: Remove one of the member's push tokens (e.g. on logout/uninstall). 404 when the token is not registered for this member — a cross-member token is treated identically, so the response never reveals another member's tokens.

404.description: Token not registered for this member.

get.description: Paginated payment history for the authenticated member, scoped to the tenant + member (a member never sees another's payments). Degrades to ``stale: true`` with the last cached page if the payment service is unavailable — it never 5xxes.

get.summary: Issue a download URL for the tenant app
get.description: Returns a short-lived presigned URL (Android) or the store link (iOS, D-6) for the tenant's stable app release (ARP-10). Auth required — anonymous callers get 401, so an unauthenticated visitor can read the listing (GET /public/app) but never download. The tenant is resolved server-side; the binary key is rebuilt from it, so a member never reaches another tenant's release.

404.description: No release available for the requested platform.

get.description:
List watchlist entries enriched with content metadata.

Each row is joined against ``content_items`` so the dashboard can
render posters/titles instead of UUIDs (#69). Missing content
(soft-deleted catalog entry) returns the row with ``title``,
``cover_url`` and ``slug`` ``null`` — backward compatible.

parameters.name: bucket

schema.description: Filter by save destination. Omit to return every bucket.
schema.title: Bucket

parameters.description: Filter by save destination. Omit to return every bucket.

schema.title: Response List Entries Api V1 Me Watchlist Get

404.description: Watchlist feature is disabled for this tenant.
Watchlist feature is disabled for this tenant.

parameters.name: bucket

schema.description: Specific bucket to remove from. Omit to remove from every bucket the member had this item in (idempotent 'remove everywhere').
schema.title: Bucket

parameters.description: Specific bucket to remove from. Omit to remove from every bucket the member had this item in (idempotent 'remove everywhere').

404.description: Watchlist entry does not exist for this member.

get.description: The authors the member follows (newest first).

schema.title: Response List Follows Api V1 Me Follows Get

put.description:
Follow an author by slug. Idempotent — re-following is a no-op that
still returns ``following: true``.

404.description: Author not found for this tenant.

delete.description:
Unfollow an author by slug. 404 when the author does not exist for
the tenant OR the member was not following them.

404.description: Author not found for this tenant.

schema.title: Response List Devices Api V1 Me Devices Get

404.description: Device-concurrency feature is disabled for this tenant.
Device-concurrency feature is disabled for this tenant.

409.description: Member is at the maximum number of registered devices.

404.description: Device id does not belong to this member.
Parental controls are disabled for this tenant.
Parental controls are disabled for this tenant.

schema.title: Response Verify Pin Api V1 Me Parental Verify Pin Post

401.description: Submitted PIN does not match the stored hash.

404.description: Parental PIN has not been configured for this member.

schema.title: Response Home Rails Api V1 Me Rails Get

get.description:
Editorial home rails for the dashboard's catalogue surface.

The shape is stable: callers always receive a ``rails`` array.
Membership is opt-in — anonymous calls succeed and just omit
``continue_watching``. ``surface`` is reserved for future
horizontal expansion (``catalog``, ``live``); v1 only honours
``home``.

When the tenant has curated rails saved via ``/admin/rails`` (and
at least one is enabled), the response mirrors that curation —
operator order, operator titles, ``source="curated"``. Tenants
that never touched the admin keep the legacy algorithmic
composition (``source="default"``), so this is opt-in per tenant
with zero migration.

Caching is intentionally deferred: the queries are tenant-scoped
indexed reads and the dashboard already short-caches the SSR
fetch. A 60s Valkey layer is the obvious next refinement once
rail traffic is observable in production.

409.description: Slug already in use for this tenant.

schema.title: Response List People Api V1 People Get

404.description: Person not found for this tenant.

409.description: Slug already in use for this tenant.

schema.title: Response List Shows Api V1 Shows Get

404.description: Show not found for this tenant.
Show not found for this tenant.

schema.title: Response List Seasons Api V1 Shows  Slug  Seasons Get

404.description: Show not found for this tenant.

post.description:
Create an episode under a show + season pair.

Returns 404 SEASON_NOT_FOUND when the show or season doesn't exist
so the dashboard can branch deterministically. Slug uniqueness is
enforced per-season at the DB layer.

404.description: Season not found for this show.

409.description: Slug already in use for this tenant.

get.description: List episodes of a season in canonical order (number ASC).

schema.title: Response List Episodes Api V1 Shows  Slug  Seasons  Season Number  Episodes Get

404.description: Season not found for this show.

get.description: Fetch an episode by id with show context + 2-item up_next list.

404.description: Episode not found for this tenant.

get.description:
Resolve a legacy WordPress path to its Atlas target.

Consumed by the dashboard middleware as a fallback when a request
path is not matched by ``tenant_settings.redirects.static``. Returns
404 when the path has no mapping so the middleware can let the
request fall through to its 404 handler.

Trailing-slash tolerance: WordPress routes ship with trailing
slashes (``/foo/``), but Next.js auto-normalises ``/foo/`` →
``/foo`` with a 308 before middleware sees the request. Without
tolerance, every legacy lookup misses on the post-308 path. We
therefore re-try the toggled-slash variant when the literal
lookup misses, so the operator can seed entries either way and
both shapes resolve identically.

404.description: Path has no mapping in the tenant's legacy_url_map.

parameters.name: status

schema.description: Filter the catalog by content status. Defaults to ``published`` so the public surface stays back-compat. For "coming soon" surfaces prefer ``coming_soon=true`` (mutually exclusive with this filter); passing ``scheduled`` lists only explicitly scheduled items.
schema.title: Status

parameters.description: Filter the catalog by content status. Defaults to ``published`` so the public surface stays back-compat. For "coming soon" surfaces prefer ``coming_soon=true`` (mutually exclusive with this filter); passing ``scheduled`` lists only explicitly scheduled items.

parameters.name: sort

schema.description: Catalog ordering. ``recent`` (default) keeps the existing behavior; ``popular`` orders by rating engagement; ``title-asc`` orders alphabetically; ``rating-desc`` orders by rating with NULLS LAST; ``release-asc`` orders by ``metadata.release_date`` ascending with NULLS LAST (coming-soon titles trail).
schema.title: Sort

parameters.description: Catalog ordering. ``recent`` (default) keeps the existing behavior; ``popular`` orders by rating engagement; ``title-asc`` orders alphabetically; ``rating-desc`` orders by rating with NULLS LAST; ``release-asc`` orders by ``metadata.release_date`` ascending with NULLS LAST (coming-soon titles trail).

parameters.name: kind

schema.description: Restrict the catalog to a single content kind (``book``, ``video``, ``episode``, ``live``, ``podcast``, ``article``).
schema.title: Kind

parameters.description: Restrict the catalog to a single content kind (``book``, ``video``, ``episode``, ``live``, ``podcast``, ``article``).

parameters.name: genre

schema.description: Filter the catalog to items whose ``metadata.tags`` intersect any of the supplied genre slugs (OR semantics). Repeat the param to combine genres.
schema.title: Genre

parameters.description: Filter the catalog to items whose ``metadata.tags`` intersect any of the supplied genre slugs (OR semantics). Repeat the param to combine genres.

parameters.name: coming_soon

schema.description: List items a "coming soon" surface should show: ``scheduled`` items plus ``published`` items with no downloadable assets yet (the same shape the item detail page locks as 'Disponible próximamente'). Mutually exclusive with ``status``.
schema.title: Coming Soon

parameters.description: List items a "coming soon" surface should show: ``scheduled`` items plus ``published`` items with no downloadable assets yet (the same shape the item detail page locks as 'Disponible próximamente'). Mutually exclusive with ``status``.

parameters.name: surface

schema.description: Platform availability filter (AM-27). ``app`` hides ``web_only`` items; ``web`` hides ``app_only`` items. Omitted ⇒ no filter (all items). Items without an ``availability`` value are treated as available everywhere (backward-compatible).
schema.title: Surface

parameters.description: Platform availability filter (AM-27). ``app`` hides ``web_only`` items; ``web`` hides ``app_only`` items. Omitted ⇒ no filter (all items). Items without an ``availability`` value are treated as available everywhere (backward-compatible).

parameters.name: has_audio

schema.description: Filter by audio playability (atlas-web W2). ``true`` keeps only items playable as audio (``kind == 'audio'`` OR a real mp3/m4a asset — the same derivation as the ``has_audio`` card flag); ``false`` keeps only non-audio items. Omitted ⇒ no filter (response byte-identical to the pre-filter shape). Filtered server-side so ``total`` and pagination stay correct.
schema.title: Has Audio

parameters.description: Filter by audio playability (atlas-web W2). ``true`` keeps only items playable as audio (``kind == 'audio'`` OR a real mp3/m4a asset — the same derivation as the ``has_audio`` card flag); ``false`` keeps only non-audio items. Omitted ⇒ no filter (response byte-identical to the pre-filter shape). Filtered server-side so ``total`` and pagination stay correct.

get.description:
Catalog headline counts for the current tenant (atlas-web W1).

All three tallies are scoped to the resolved tenant and to
``status == "published"`` only:

* ``total`` — every published item.
* ``audiobooks`` — published items playable as audio, using the SAME
  derivation as the ``has_audio`` card flag (``kind == "audio"`` OR a
  real mp3/m4a asset — the shared ``ContentRepository._has_audio_condition``,
  the SQL twin of :func:`_has_audio`).
* ``books`` — published items whose ``kind`` is a reading kind
  (:data:`_READING_KINDS`).

``books`` and ``audiobooks`` are intentionally NON-exclusive: a title
with a reading kind AND an audio asset counts in both, so their sum
can exceed ``total``.

Full detail for one library item — byline, genre, formats, related.

Serves ``published`` and ``scheduled`` (coming-soon) items so the
detail screen renders an upcoming title with its release date and a
locked download state. Draft/archived items 404 with the stable
``CONTENT_NOT_FOUND`` code. Tenant-scoped via the slug lookup, so a
slug from another tenant never resolves.

The embedded ``related`` rail uses the SAME ranking as the standalone
``/items/{slug}/related`` endpoint (F1c: genre tier first, then
same-kind fill, each ordered rating desc) so both surfaces agree —
only the size differs (``related_limit`` default 8, cap 24, unchanged).
The earlier recency-only embedded ranking is superseded.

404.description: Item not found, draft, or archived for this tenant.

get.description:
Surface the tenant's hero pick.

Resolves ``tenant.config.featured_item_id`` first; falls back to the
most recently published item if the configured id is missing,
archived, or never matched. Returns ``{"item": null}`` when the
catalog has no published items so the dashboard can render an
explicit empty state.

Related rail for a published item (atlas-app redesign F1c).

Same-tenant published items, excluding the seed: same-genre matches
first, then same-kind fill, each tier ordered ``rating_avg`` desc
(NULLS LAST). Default 6, capped at 12 — the app contract; the earlier
recency-only ranking (default 10, cap 50) is superseded. Cards reuse
the shared :class:`ContentSummary` serializer.

404.description: Seed item not found or not published.

get.description:
Catalog genres derived from ``content_item.metadata.tags``.

Aggregation runs in Python (not SQL) so the call is portable across
Postgres and SQLite. The published-only filter caps the worst-case
cost; tenants with ≫10k items should switch to a `pg_trgm` materialised
view (out of scope for v1).

``has_audio`` narrows the underlying row set (not the aggregation), so
both the counts *and* the visible chip set scope to audiobooks — no
zero-audiobook genres leak into the ``/audiolibros`` sidebar.

parameters.name: has_audio

schema.description: Scope genre counts to audiobook-only (``true``) or non-audio (``false``) — the same ``has_audio`` derivation as the catalog filter and the audiobook card flag. Powers the ``/audiolibros`` sidebar so only genres with audiobooks appear. Omitted ⇒ global counts (response byte-identical to the pre-filter shape).
schema.title: Has Audio

parameters.description: Scope genre counts to audiobook-only (``true``) or non-audio (``false``) — the same ``has_audio`` derivation as the catalog filter and the audiobook card flag. Powers the ``/audiolibros`` sidebar so only genres with audiobooks appear. Omitted ⇒ global counts (response byte-identical to the pre-filter shape).

get.description:
Full-text search over the published catalog, grouped by kind.

Postgres path uses the FTS GIN index from migration 0014 and emits
a server-rendered highlight (``ts_headline``) wrapping matched
tokens in ``<mark>`` so the dashboard can render them as HTML
safely.

SQLite test path uses an ILIKE fallback over title and
metadata.author and produces an equivalent ``<mark>``-wrapped
highlight in Python, keeping the response contract identical for
the dashboard regardless of backing store.

Return the public rating aggregate for a content item.

The ``rating_avg`` / ``rating_count`` columns on ``content_items``
are kept in lock-step by ``ReviewRepository._refresh_denorm`` so
this endpoint is a single-row read; the per-rating distribution is
a small GROUP BY on the ``reviews`` table.

404.description: Item not found or not published.

get.description:
Paginate published reviews for a content item.

Both ``sort=recent`` and ``sort=helpful`` order by ``created_at``
desc in v1 — a ``helpful_count`` column is a post-redesign
refinement (see REDESIGN_PLAN.md cut C8). The accepted-but-aliased
parameter keeps the dashboard contract stable.

404.description: Item not found or not published.

get.description:
Public author profile: typed bio/born/country + published works.

Tenant-scoped ``by_slug`` resolution (404 ``PERSON_NOT_FOUND``). Works
are matched by ``metadata.people`` slug or the ``metadata.author``
byline — NOT ``cast_crew`` — so a library author's books actually
surface. Distinct from ``/people/{slug}/credits`` (cast_crew, grouped),
which is left untouched for backward compatibility.

404.description: Person not found for this tenant.

get.description:
Filmography grouped by series (default) or kind.

Iterates published items in Python and filters those whose
``metadata.cast_crew[*].person_id`` matches the resolved person id.
The Python-side scan keeps the call portable across Postgres and
SQLite without dialect-specific JSON containment operators.

404.description: Person not found for this tenant.

get.description:
Public subscription plan list, filtered by billing period.

Plans are sourced from ``tenant.config.plans`` (operator-managed
via /admin/settings). The endpoint is read-only and cached at the
edge by callers; the actual subscribe action is brokered through
the entitlement provider, never this router.

``discount_pct`` is computed by the operator and surfaced from
``tenant.config.plans_discount_pct`` so the dashboard can render
"Save N%" badges on the yearly toggle without re-deriving math.

Return the EPG slot grid for the live archetype.

``from`` defaults to "now"; ``to`` defaults to ``from + 24h``. The
server caps the window at 24h to keep the response bounded — a
longer EPG export is a deliberate non-goal for v1 (see cut C4).
Slots are grouped by ``channel_id`` and serialised channel-major
so the dashboard can render the grid without re-grouping.

Anonymised "social proof" stream powering the home toast.

v1 surfaces only ``subscribe`` events derived from plan-scoped
entitlements created in the last 24 hours. Each event's actor is
hashed via ``HMAC-SHA256(tenant.activity_feed_secret, …)`` so the
raw ``member_id`` never crosses the tenant boundary
(REDESIGN_PLAN.md §14.4.5). PII fields (email, country, exact
plan name) are deliberately omitted.

schema.title: Response Sitemap Index Api V1 Public Sitemap Index Get

get.description: Store-style listing + per-platform availability for the tenant's app (ARP-09). Public (no auth); 404 when the section is disabled. Carries no download URLs — those are issued, presigned and short-lived, by GET /me/app/download.

404.description: App section is not enabled for this tenant.

get.description:
Return up to ``limit`` published-item suggestions for ``q``.

* ``q`` is normalised to its stripped form before validation, so a
  query of ``"   "`` is treated as empty.
* Empty queries return ``SEARCH_QUERY_EMPTY`` (400) rather than 422
  so the dashboard renders the same "type to search" empty-state
  regardless of how the input was cleared.
* Queries longer than :data:`MAX_SEARCH_Q_LEN` raise
  ``SEARCH_QUERY_TOO_LONG`` (400) — Query upper bound is set one
  higher so we own the error code instead of FastAPI's 422 envelope.

post.summary: Upsert a member by email and return a fresh sign-in URL
post.description:
Idempotent on ``(tenant_id, email)``.

The ``created`` flag is informational; callers should treat both
states the same. The returned ``sign_in_url`` is single-use and
expires per ``magic_link_ttl_seconds`` — typically 15 minutes.

404.description: Tenant tied to the key no longer exists

post.summary: Issue a fresh sign-in URL for an existing member
post.description:
Returns a fresh ephemeral sign-in URL.

Cross-tenant lookups return 404 (not 403) so a leaked key cannot
enumerate member ids across tenants.

404.description: Member not found in the key's tenant

AccessFlagsSchema.title: AccessFlagsSchema
AccessFlagsSchema.description:
Per-item access policy hints surfaced to the dashboard.

These are advisory flags rendered on the content detail screen;
actual access enforcement still goes through the entitlement +
delivery strategy stack.

AchievementCatalogItem.title: AchievementCatalogItem
AchievementCatalogItem.description: A medal in the member's achievement catalog (locked or unlocked).

AdminContentItemDetail.title: AdminContentItemDetail
AdminContentItemDetail.description: Full detail payload for the content edit screen.

AdminContentItemPatch.title: AdminContentItemPatch
AdminContentItemPatch.description:
PATCH body — every field is optional; ``None`` means leave alone.

The shape is deliberately narrower than ``ContentItemUpdate`` (the
legacy non-admin PATCH model) so the admin UI cannot, say, mutate
fields the redesign hasn't surfaced yet.

AdminContentItemRow.title: AdminContentItemRow
AdminContentItemRow.description: Compact list row used by the admin catalog table.

AppAnalyticsConfig.title: AppAnalyticsConfig
AppAnalyticsConfig.description:
Per-tenant analytics capture policy (AAN-01) — mirrors ``AppReleases``.

Additive sub-object of ``AppConfig`` (``extra="allow"``) so it rides
``/tenants/by-host`` with no migration; ``None`` for every pre-existing
tenant ⇒ capture stays off (default-safe, D-3). ``extra="forbid"``
because the dashboard admin is the only writer.

The measurement *tool* is decoupled via Strategy (D-1): ``provider`` is
the discriminator the backend factory reads to pick a sink/reader. This
iteration only ``google_analytics`` is wired; ``noop`` (the default)
drops every event before any external call. The discriminator value is
config/code — never a UI label (brand rule).

AppConfig.title: AppConfig
AppConfig.description:
Per-tenant native-app configuration (AM-21).

Surfaced inside ``/tenants/by-host`` → ``config.app`` (additive — a
tenant without it gets ``None`` and the app falls back to the
archetype's app-feature defaults). Operators edit it from the
dashboard "App / Móvil" section. ``extra=allow`` keeps it
forward-compatible with flags the app learns about before the schema
does.

Resolution precedence (documented for the client): an explicit value
in ``features``/``exclusive`` wins over the archetype default; a key
absent here inherits the archetype default
(``app.archetypes.registry.resolve_app_features``).

AppDownloadView.title: AppDownloadView
AppDownloadView.description:
``GET /me/app/download`` — a short-lived presigned URL (or store
link for iOS). Returned as JSON (not a cached 302) so it never lands in
browser history (audit H-1).

AppFreeQuota.title: AppFreeQuota
AppFreeQuota.description:
Free-tier quota knobs for the freemium policy (AFR-01, D-5b/D-15/D-19).

``books_per_day`` is the hero of the model: the number of *distinct*
premium titles a free member may open in full (read in the in-app
reader **or** download) per window — counted by distinct ``content_id``,
not requests (so read+download of the same title = one slot). ``None``
⇒ no book quota. ``preview_pages`` caps the EPUB in-app preview of a
not-yet-unlocked premium title (does NOT consume a slot, D-19).
``downloads_per_day``/``reads_per_month`` are legacy/compat knobs.
``keeps_unlocked`` (Audible "it's yours") makes a spent slot grant a
permanent per-item entitlement (D-15). ``rollover`` is v2 (unused).

AppFreemium.title: AppFreemium
AppFreemium.description:
Per-tenant freemium policy (AFR-01) — mirrors ``AppReleases``.

Additive sub-object of ``AppConfig`` (``extra="allow"``) so it rides
``/tenants/by-host`` with no migration; ``None`` (or ``enabled=false``)
for every pre-existing tenant ⇒ no gating (full access, no regression —
R-1/R-5). ``extra="forbid"`` because the dashboard admin is the only
writer and the app renders the fields verbatim. The policy carries NO
secrets and NO vendor names — only declarative gates/quotas/copy keys.

AppFreemiumGate.title: AppFreemiumGate
AppFreemiumGate.description:
A single declarative freemium gate (AFR-01, D-6).

``feature`` is the capability key the app/backend gate against (e.g.
``premium_content``, ``offline_download``, ``early_access``).
``required_plan`` references a ``config.plans`` slug — an active
``scope=plan`` entitlement with that slug unlocks the gate (the
enforcement NEVER hardcodes a slug, D-13). ``free_limit`` is an optional
free allowance before the gate engages (``None`` ⇒ hard gate).

AppFreemiumTrial.title: AppFreemiumTrial
AppFreemiumTrial.description:
Free trial of a paid plan (AFR-01, D-14 — Spotify/Audible lever).

When ``enabled`` and ``plan_ref``/``days`` are set, the trial grant path
issues a temporary ``Entitlement(scope="plan", scope_ref=plan_ref,
expires_at=now+days, granted_by="trial")`` that unlocks like VIP until it
passively expires (no revoke job). ``once_per_member`` dedups by any
existing ``granted_by="trial"`` row for the (tenant, member, plan).

AppGenreRailsConfig.title: AppGenreRailsConfig
AppGenreRailsConfig.description:
Per-tenant editorial-home genre-rails knobs (AM-05) — mirrors ``AppReaderConfig``.

Additive sub-object of ``AppConfig``; ``None`` for every pre-existing tenant
⇒ the app applies the safe defaults (``initial=4``, ``step=4``,
``prefetch_px=800``, ``page_size=12``, ``max=20``) — no migration, zero
regression. Every field is **optional** so the dashboard can clear one knob
back to the app default independently; the app's ``resolveGenreRailsConfig``
is tolerant and clamps ``initial``/``step`` to ``max``. ``extra="forbid"``
because the dashboard admin is the only writer.

Bounds keep a config from degrading the home: counts are capped at 100 and
the prefetch trigger at 10000px so a bad value can't request giant pages or
mount an absurd number of rails.

AppPaywall.title: AppPaywall
AppPaywall.description:
Paywall presentation policy (AFR-01, D-16). UX only — the binding
gate is server-side (D-3). ``style="soft"`` shows a meter + countdown
(Medium/NYT); ``hard`` is a dry wall. ``headline_key`` is an i18n key
(no embedded copy); ``cta_plan_slug`` is the promoted ``config.plans``
slug.

AppPlansLayout.title: AppPlansLayout
AppPlansLayout.description:
Pricing-landing presentation (AFR-04, D-17 — anchoring). UX hints
only; all plans come from ``config.plans`` (no hardcode, D-13).

AppReaderConfig.title: AppReaderConfig
AppReaderConfig.description:
Per-tenant in-app reader policy (AIR-02) — mirrors ``AppReleases``.

Additive sub-object of ``AppConfig``; ``None`` for every pre-existing
tenant ⇒ the app applies the safe default (``in_app_reader=True``,
``formats=["epub","pdf"]``) — MOBI is never attempted until an operator
adds it, so existing tenants are untouched (D-2/D-3). ``extra="forbid"``
because the dashboard admin is the only writer.

AppReleases.title: AppReleases
AppReleases.description:
Storefront master toggle + listing for the per-tenant app page (ARP-08).

``enabled`` defaults ``False`` so a tenant without an explicit opt-in
never advertises an app section (backward-compatible: ``GET /public/app``
→ 404 and the navbar item stays hidden until an operator flips it).

AppReleasesListing.title: AppReleasesListing
AppReleasesListing.description: Store-listing copy for the app storefront (operator-editable, ARP-08).

AppScreenshot.title: AppScreenshot
AppScreenshot.description: One store screenshot: an https image URL + its (a11y-mandatory) alt. ``alt`` is stored alongside the URL so the storefront renders a meaningful ```` the operator authored, not a generated placeholder (audit M-6 / ARP-11 a11y).

AppUpdate.title: AppUpdate
AppUpdate.description:
Per-tenant app update policy (AUG-01) — mirrors ``AppReleases``.

Additive sub-object of ``AppConfig`` (``extra="allow"``) so it rides
``/tenants/by-host`` with no migration; ``None`` for every pre-existing
tenant ⇒ the app shows nothing (fail-open, D-4). ``extra="forbid"``
because the dashboard admin is the only writer and the app renders the
fields verbatim.

The backend stays laxo on semver (accepts any string up to 64 chars):
the app is the authority and fails open on an unparseable version, so a
typo can never brick the client. The one hard invariant: an *enabled*
gate must have a web destination, otherwise the CTA would route nowhere.

AppUpdateUrls.title: AppUpdateUrls
AppUpdateUrls.description:
Per-platform download destinations for the update gate (AUG-01).

All https-only (``HttpsUrlStr``) so a hand-edited config can never route
the "Update" CTA to an http/data:/relative target (anti-phishing). The
app prefers the platform-specific URL and falls back to ``web``:
store-distributed builds point ``android``/``ios`` at the store listing;
sideload/enterprise builds point ``web`` at the download page (D-8/R-2).

AudioChapterIn.title: AudioChapterIn
AudioChapterIn.description:
One audiobook chapter in the merge-preserve upsert payload.

Mirror of the public ``AudioChapterOut`` shape (``public.py``) so the
write contract matches what the item detail derives back
(``_derive_item_audio``). ``duration_sec`` is optional — a chapter can
be catalogued before its runtime is known; the public serializer sums
only the non-null durations.

AudioChapterOut.title: AudioChapterOut
AudioChapterOut.description: A single audiobook chapter as surfaced on the public detail.

AudioUpsert.title: AudioUpsert
AudioUpsert.description:
Request body for ``PUT /content/items/{id}/audio`` (AUD-01).

Sets ONLY ``narrator`` + ``audio_chapters`` on the item's metadata;
the endpoint merge-preserves every other key (assets, cover, …) so
the upsert can never drop a downloadable asset (D-1). Orders must be
unique and contiguous ``1..N`` — the endpoint persists them sorted so
delivery/public resolve chapters deterministically.

AuthorProfileResponse.title: AuthorProfileResponse
AuthorProfileResponse.description:
Public author profile + works (atlas-redesign v2).

``bio``/``born``/``country`` are projected from the person's free
``metadata`` JSON (derive-at-read — no schema migration). ``works`` are
the author's published items matched by structured ``metadata.people``
slug or the ``metadata.author`` byline (see
``ContentRepository.list_by_author``).

format.title: Format
format.description: Downloadable format. One of: pdf, epub, mobi, mp3, m4a

chapter.title: Chapter
chapter.description: 1-based audiobook chapter order (AUD-05). When set, the audio lands per-chapter (audio_ch_{order}) instead of the full file; must be an order already declared via PUT /audio.

BrandingSettings.title: BrandingSettings
BrandingSettings.description:
Per-tenant brand identity.

The ``primary_color`` + ``accent_color`` pair drives the existing
`--atlas-primary-color` / `--atlas-accent-color` CSS variables.
The five additional optional color fields below let an operator
override the rest of the palette (text + surfaces + hairlines)
without the dashboard hardcoding presets per slug. Each field is
nullable; the dashboard falls back to its dark-first defaults
when a value is absent.

ConfigPayments.title: ConfigPayments
ConfigPayments.description:
Per-tenant payment-provider config (AFR-05, D-18) — **NON-secret**.

Lives at ``tenants.config.payments`` (sibling of ``config.plans``, NOT
under ``config.app``) and is served verbatim by ``GET /tenants/by-host``
— so it MUST never carry ``api_key``/``webhook_secret`` (R-8). Those are
resolved server-side from env namespaced per tenant
(``ENTITLEMENT_PROVIDER_WEBHOOK_SECRET__<tenant_slug>``, fallback global).

``provider_kind`` discriminates the entitlement-provider factory:
``mock`` (default ⇒ no charge, no regression) or ``external`` (the
configured remote payment layer). ``base_url`` overrides the global
endpoint per tenant. ``plan_product_map`` maps a ``config.plans`` slug to
the provider's product id (what gets sold). The discriminator/value are
config/code — never a UI label (brand rule).

ContentAssetOut.title: ContentAssetOut
ContentAssetOut.description:
Response shape for ``POST /content/items/{id}/assets``.

The DOWNLOAD delivery strategy reads from ``key`` directly; the
dashboard surfaces ``size_bytes`` + ``uploaded_at`` so operators can
confirm the upload landed.

ContentItemUpdate.title: ContentItemUpdate
ContentItemUpdate.description:
Partial update payload. ``id``, ``tenant_id`` and ``created_at``
are deliberately absent — they are immutable once the row exists.
All other fields are optional; the handler skips ``None`` values so
callers can patch a single attribute at a time.

ContentMetadataSchema.title: ContentMetadataSchema
ContentMetadataSchema.description:
Structured view over the free-form ``ContentItem.metadata`` blob.

Validates the known keys (summary, genres, language, year, ISBN,
credits, tags, access flags) while preserving unknown keys via
``extra="allow"`` so the server-action layer can round-trip them
through ``model_dump(exclude_none=False)`` without losing data the
schema doesn't yet model (per REDESIGN_PLAN.md D4).

ContentMetrics30d.title: ContentMetrics30d
ContentMetrics30d.description:
Rolling 30-day metrics block surfaced on content detail.

Embedded into the content detail response under ``metrics`` so the
admin UI can render the sparkline + completion gauge without a
second round trip.

ContentRef.title: ContentRef
ContentRef.description:
Minimal content reference used inside ``/me/library`` payloads.

``title``/``slug``/``cover_url``/``kind`` are best-effort — when the
underlying ``content`` row was soft-deleted they come back ``null``
so the dashboard can fall back to ``content_id``.

ContentSummary.title: ContentSummary
ContentSummary.description:
Compact public shape reused by /featured, /related, /search.

Includes rating denorm so the dashboard renders ★ averages without
a second roundtrip.

CustomCodeSettings.title: CustomCodeSettings
CustomCodeSettings.description:
Operator-pasted markup injected into the public site.

``head_html`` and ``body_html`` are raw HTML blocks the public
frontend renders verbatim on tenant-facing routes only — never on
the admin / auth shells. They exist so an operator can wire their
own measurement / tag-manager snippets per tenant without an API
field per provider. Treated as trusted operator input: only roles
with ``settings.branding.write`` can set them, and the dashboard
gates injection to public routes so one tenant's markup can never
execute inside the admin surface.

EventBatch.title: EventBatch
EventBatch.description:
Ingest envelope. The batch cap is enforced in the endpoint (so the
over-cap case maps to the stable ``EVENT_BATCH_TOO_LARGE`` domain code
rather than a generic list-length Pydantic error).

EventIngestResponse.title: EventIngestResponse
EventIngestResponse.description:
``202`` response: how many events were accepted for delivery to the
active measurement tool (0 when capture is off / out of allowlist).

FeatureFlags.title: FeatureFlags
FeatureFlags.description:
Per-tenant feature toggles (see docs/atlas/14-tenant-archetypes.md).

``extra="allow"`` keeps the namespace open for the
``atlas_seo_*``, ``atlas_about_*`` and ``atlas_footer_*`` flags the
SEO recovery archetype seeds. New flags don't need a schema bump.

FooterSocialLink.title: FooterSocialLink
FooterSocialLink.description:
A social icon + link in the footer "follow us" row.

``kind`` selects the inline-SVG icon the dashboard renders; the
operator supplies the URL (no vendor handle hardcoded).

GoogleAnalyticsConfig.title: GoogleAnalyticsConfig
GoogleAnalyticsConfig.description:
Per-tenant measurement-tool property identifiers (AAN-01) — **non-secret**.

Only the public-ish identifiers live here (they ship in config, not in
the bundle): ``measurement_id`` ("G-XXXXXXX") routes the Measurement
Protocol write, ``property_id`` ("123456789") scopes the Data API read.
The real secrets — the MP ``api_secret`` and the service-account JSON —
live in the server env (``GA4_API_SECRET`` / ``GA4_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_JSON``)
and never appear in config, bootstrap, or the dashboard.

GuestCheckoutRequest.title: GuestCheckoutRequest
GuestCheckoutRequest.description:
Anonymous checkout: the buyer supplies only an email (the card is
entered on the provider's hosted page, never here). The email creates or
reuses a tenant-scoped member so the paid grant can be attributed on the
webhook. Same plan/scope fields as :class:`CheckoutRequest`.

IngestEvent.title: IngestEvent
IngestEvent.description: A single app-emitted event (generic transport — no vendor coupling).

IntegrationsSettings.title: IntegrationsSettings
IntegrationsSettings.description:
Tracking IDs storeable today; injected by the frontend later.

NEVER reference the vendor name in the field comment; see
``feedback_provider_leak_via_exception``.

ItemAudio.title: ItemAudio
ItemAudio.description:
Audiobook block on the public item detail (atlas-app redesign F1a).

Present only when the item declares ``metadata.audio_chapters`` —
items without chapters keep their payload byte-identical (L-33c
derive-at-read guard). ``total_duration_sec`` sums the non-null
chapter durations, or is ``null`` when no chapter carries one.

ItemDetail.title: ItemDetail
ItemDetail.description:
Full public detail for a single content item.

Powers the redesigned library detail screen: byline + genre eyebrow +
serif lede + format cards + metadata deflist + inline related rail.
Serves ``published`` and ``scheduled`` (coming-soon) items; everything
else 404s with ``CONTENT_NOT_FOUND``.

ItemFormat.title: ItemFormat
ItemFormat.description: A downloadable format card on the detail screen (EPUB/PDF/MOBI…).

KpiChartPoint.title: KpiChartPoint
KpiChartPoint.description: One day in the readings sparkline. Server zero-fills missing days.

KpiTopItem.title: KpiTopItem
KpiTopItem.description: Row in the "Top items today" widget on the admin home.

LibraryItemImportRequest.title: LibraryItemImportRequest
LibraryItemImportRequest.description:
Promote existing tenant content_items into canonical library_items.

``source_tenant_id`` is the tenant whose items become the canonical
origin (epublibre for the first library). ``content_item_ids`` limits
the import; empty = all of that tenant's items.

MediaSupplyConfigOut.title: MediaSupplyConfigOut
MediaSupplyConfigOut.description: Admin read surface — never exposes the secret values.

MediaSupplyConfigPatch.title: MediaSupplyConfigPatch
MediaSupplyConfigPatch.description: Admin write surface. Omitted secret fields preserve the stored value.

PaymentsPage.title: PaymentsPage
PaymentsPage.description:
A page of the member's payment history.

Degrades gracefully: when the upstream payment service is
unavailable the endpoint returns ``stale=true`` with the last known
page (from cache) instead of a 5xx, so the app keeps rendering the
history. ``last_synced_at`` tells the client how fresh the data is.

PersonRefSchema.title: PersonRefSchema
PersonRefSchema.description:
Reference to a person credited on a content item.

Used inside ``ContentMetadataSchema.people`` for authors,
translators, directors, narrators, etc. ``id`` is optional so the
admin can list credits before any normalised person record exists.

PlaybackChapter.title: PlaybackChapter
PlaybackChapter.description: Echo of the resolved audiobook chapter (atlas-app redesign F1b).

PublicAppView.title: PublicAppView
PublicAppView.description:
``GET /public/app`` — listing + availability, gated by the master
toggle (404 when disabled, never ``enabled=false``).

PublicPlatformInfo.title: PublicPlatformInfo
PublicPlatformInfo.description:
Per-platform availability for the public storefront — metadata only.

No object key, no sha256, no PRIVATE binary URL (audit M-3/H-4).
``store_url`` is the PUBLIC App Store/TestFlight link (iOS, D-6) — safe
to expose since it is a public destination, not a gated binary; the
Android binary still flows only through the login-gated presign.
``version_code`` is null for iOS (store-distributed).

PublicStats.title: PublicStats
PublicStats.description:
Catalog headline counts for the web landing (atlas-web W1).

``total`` is every published item; ``books`` and ``audiobooks`` are
NOT a partition — a title carrying both a reading kind and an mp3/m4a
asset counts in both — so ``books + audiobooks`` may exceed ``total``.

PushTokenIn.title: PushTokenIn
PushTokenIn.description:
Register-a-device-token request.

``token`` is the opaque platform push handle (e.g.
``ExponentPushToken[...]``). It is treated as sensitive: never
logged in full. ``device_fingerprint`` lets the app reconcile a
token with one of the member's known devices.

PushTokenOut.title: PushTokenOut
PushTokenOut.description: Public view of a stored push token — never echoes the token value.

RailsCopy.title: RailsCopy
RailsCopy.description:
Editorial home-rail titles, keyed by the stable rail ``key`` that
``/public/rails`` and ``/me/rails`` emit (no English title on the
wire — the dashboard resolves the title from here).

ReadingsChartPoint.title: ReadingsChartPoint
ReadingsChartPoint.description: One day in a readings timeseries. Server-side zero-fills gaps.

SeoCrawlerPolicy.title: SeoCrawlerPolicy
SeoCrawlerPolicy.description:
Per-tenant crawler directives surfaced in ``robots.txt``.

``ai_bots`` is the coarse intent flag operators/ops read for the
edge decision; the real AI block lives at the CDN zone, not in DB
(plan D-5). ``disallow_agents`` is the explicit per-agent list
the dashboard emits as ``Disallow: /`` — Googlebot/Bingbot are never
disallowed regardless of what lands here.

SiteVerification.title: SiteVerification
SiteVerification.description:
Operator-pasted verification tokens for search engine consoles.

Stored on ``tenant_settings.seo`` (JSONB). Each value is the
``content`` attribute the provider gives you in their console — NOT
the full ``<meta>`` element. The dashboard wraps the token in a
``<meta>`` tag via Next.js's ``metadata.verification`` API so
attribute escaping is owned by the framework.

SubscriptionOut.title: SubscriptionOut
SubscriptionOut.description:
Active plan-scope entitlement view for the dashboard's
SubscriptionCard. ``subscription`` is null when the member has no
active plan grant — the card hides itself in that case.

SubscriptionStatusView.title: SubscriptionStatusView
SubscriptionStatusView.description: Sync health for one subscription — drives the dashboard (F5).

SyncPolicy.title: SyncPolicy
SyncPolicy.description: Per-subscription policy controlling how fichas materialize.

TenantCopy.title: TenantCopy
TenantCopy.description:
Operator-editable, fan-facing UI copy for the library archetype.

Every namespace defaults to an empty sub-model so reads of a tenant
with no overrides never raise; every leaf is optional so the
dashboard falls through to its localized default per string.

TenantLegacyKindPath.title: TenantLegacyKindPath
TenantLegacyKindPath.description:
An old ``kind_path`` kept alive after an admin renamed it.

The dashboard resolves the legacy segment first; if it matches it
serves a 301 to the new canonical URL. ``expires_at`` is set by the
PATCH handler (90 days from the rename); a scheduled job removes
expired entries.

TenantUrlRouting.title: TenantUrlRouting
TenantUrlRouting.description:
Per-tenant URL routing for public item pages.

Backwards-compatible by construction: an empty ``kind_paths`` means
the dashboard keeps serving ``/items/<slug>``. The contract becomes
active only when an admin sets at least one mapping.

kind_paths.title: Kind Paths
kind_paths.description: Replacement value for the active mapping. Pass null to leave the existing mapping untouched.

TenantUrlRoutingPatch.title: TenantUrlRoutingPatch
TenantUrlRoutingPatch.description:
Desired-state patch for ``TenantConfig.url_routing``.

The handler diffs against the persisted state: any path that is
being renamed or removed is moved into ``kind_paths_legacy`` with a
90-day TTL so existing backlinks keep resolving via a 301 on the
dashboard. Adding a new mapping is a no-op for the legacy dict.

TrialResponse.title: TrialResponse
TrialResponse.description: Outcome of a free-trial activation (AFR-01, D-14).

WatchlistBucket.title: WatchlistBucket
WatchlistBucket.description:
Save-destination discriminator on a watchlist row (#68).

Stored as a plain ``VARCHAR(16)`` in Postgres so adding a new
bucket later doesn't require evolving a PG enum type — the
application is the only writer and validates against this set
before insert.
